concept: mkbitmap lowpass filter: smoothing foreground detail before tracing
source_excerpt: mkbitmap.c: a standalone program for converting greymaps to bitmaps while optionally applying the following enhancements: highpass filter (evening out background gradients), lowpass filter (smoothing foreground details) ... apply lowpass filter (an approximate Gaussian blur) to greymap. Lambda is the standard deviation of the kernel
implication: Potrace's companion mkbitmap pre-processes the raster with a lowpass (approximate Gaussian) blur to smooth foreground detail and anti-alias noise BEFORE thresholding, so the tracer forms cleaner regions from anti-aliased edges. This is a pre-tracer source-denoise step, not a tracer parameter.

param_mapping.rationale: There is NO vtracer parameter for input blur. This maps onto the vectorizer's existing _presmoothed_source() step (currently a size-3 median filter applied to soft-shaded sources only). mkbitmap's lowpass supports extending/tuning that pre-smoothing (Gaussian sigma, or applying it to noisy scans, not just soft-shaded art) as an isolated pre-tracer factor. This is a pipeline knob, not a vtracer kwarg.

hypothesis: Applying a light lowpass/median presmooth to noisy or anti-aliased sources before vtracer (as mkbitmap does) will let filter_speckle stay low while still producing clean regions, raising color_fidelity/ssim, because the noise is removed in raster space instead of being over-filtered in vector space.
